questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Choose the correct preposition: 'The ore sample was analysed ____ traces of gold.'
questions.solution:
**Concept.** 'Analyse for' is the idiomatic collocation when testing for the presence of something.

**Working.** We test a sample 'for' a substance; the other prepositions do not fit this sense.

**Answer.** for.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A triangle is divided by a single line from its apex to the midpoint of its base (figure). Count the total number of triangles in the figure.

figure.caption: Triangle with one median
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 150' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><polygon points='40,120 160,120 100,30' fill='#bfdbfe' stroke='#475569'/><line x1='100' y1='30' x2='100' y2='120' stroke='#475569'/><text x='35' y='140' font-size='8'>count the triangles</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Count small and combined triangles.

**Working.** Two small right triangles, plus the whole large triangle, give $2+1=3$.

**Answer.** $3$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: On the clock face shown, the time is $3{:}00$. Compute the smaller angle between the hour and minute hands (degrees).

figure.caption: Clock at 3:00
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 170 175' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><circle cx='85' cy='80' r='60' fill='#e5e7eb' stroke='#475569'/><line x1='85' y1='80' x2='85' y2='28' stroke='#1f2937' stroke-width='3'/><line x1='85' y1='80' x2='133' y2='80' stroke='#dc2626' stroke-width='2'/><text x='55' y='160' font-size='8'>time = 3:00</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Each hour mark is $30^\circ$ apart.

**Working.**
$$3\text{ hours}\times30^\circ=90^\circ$$

**Answer.** $90^\circ$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Compute the area of the triangle shown with base $6$ and height $4$.

figure.caption: Triangle base and height
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 150' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><polygon points='40,120 160,120 100,40' fill='#bfdbfe' stroke='#475569'/><line x1='100' y1='120' x2='100' y2='40' stroke='#dc2626' stroke-dasharray='3 2'/><text x='90' y='135' font-size='8'>base 6</text><text x='105' y='85' font-size='8'>height 4</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Area $=\tfrac{1}{2}bh$.

**Working.**
$$A=\tfrac{1}{2}(6)(4)=12$$

**Answer.** $12$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The area of a rectangle of length $12$ and width $5$ is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Area of rectangle $=\text{length}\times\text{width}$.

**Working.** $12\times 5 = 60$.

**Answer.** $60$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem:
Read the passage and answer.

'The introduction of automated haulage trucks reduced accidents in the pit by 40%. However, it also displaced a number of human drivers, prompting the company to launch a retraining programme.'

It can be inferred that the company:
options: sought to address the impact of automation on its workforce
abandoned automation because of the job losses
increased the number of human drivers it employed
ignored the workers displaced by automation
questions.solution:
**Concept.** An inference must follow logically from stated facts.

**Working.** Launching a retraining programme in response to displaced drivers shows the company addressed automation's workforce impact.

**Answer.** sought to address the impact of automation on its workforce.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Pipe A fills a tank in $10$ hours while pipe B empties it in $15$ hours. With both open, the tank fills in
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Subtract the emptying rate from the filling rate.

**Working.** $\dfrac{1}{10}-\dfrac{1}{15}=\dfrac{3-2}{30}=\dfrac{1}{30}$, so $30$ hours.

**Answer.** $30$ hours.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Fill in the blank: 'The auditor's ____ examination revealed discrepancies that others had overlooked.'
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Detecting overlooked discrepancies requires a very thorough, careful examination.

**Working.** 'Scrupulous' means diligent and exact; 'cursory' (hasty) would miss details.

**Answer.** scrupulous.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: In a queue, a person is $15$th from the front and $10$th from the back. The total number of people in the queue is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Total $=$ front rank $+$ back rank $-1$.

**Working.** $15+10-1=24$.

**Answer.** $24$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A card is drawn at random from a standard $52$-card deck. The probability that it is an ace is _____ (round to two decimals).
questions.solution:
**Concept.** There are $4$ aces in $52$ cards.

**Working.** $\dfrac{4}{52}=\dfrac{1}{13}\approx 0.08$.

**Answer.** $\approx 0.08$.

questions.subject: Global & Regional Environmental Issues
questions.stem: A source emits $12\,\text{t}$ of a greenhouse gas. Reading the gas's GWP₁₀₀ from the figure, its CO₂-equivalent emission is _____ t CO₂-e.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 130" width="100%" style="max-width:300px;height:auto"><rect x="40" y="36" width="220" height="56" rx="6" fill="rgba(217,119,6,0.12)" stroke="#1e293b"/><text x="150" y="58" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">the gas</text><text x="150" y="80" font-size="12" text-anchor="middle" fill="#dc2626">GWP₁₀₀ = 28</text><text x="150" y="112" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">CO₂-e = mass × GWP</text></svg>
figure.caption: Global-warming potential of the gas marked on the diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** CO₂-e = mass × GWP, with the GWP read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$12\times28=336.$$

**Answer.** $336$

questions.subject: Environmental Management & Ethics
questions.stem: Which statement about the EIA process in India is correct?
options: Public consultation is a mandatory stage for Category A projects
EIA is optional for all projects
Scoping precedes the identification of the project

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 360 90" width="100%" style="max-width:360px;height:auto"><rect x="8" y="28" width="60" height="34" rx="4" fill="rgba(37,99,235,0.08)" stroke="#1e293b"/><text x="38" y="49" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">Screening</text><path d="M68 45 l10 0 m-4 -3 l4 3 -4 3" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.1" fill="none"/><rect x="78" y="28" width="60" height="34" rx="4" fill="rgba(37,99,235,0.08)" stroke="#1e293b"/><text x="108" y="49" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">Scoping</text><path d="M138 45 l10 0 m-4 -3 l4 3 -4 3" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.1" fill="none"/><rect x="148" y="28" width="60" height="34" rx="4" fill="rgba(37,99,235,0.08)" stroke="#1e293b"/><text x="178" y="44" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">Impact</text><text x="178" y="55" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">prediction</text><path d="M208 45 l10 0 m-4 -3 l4 3 -4 3" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.1" fill="none"/><rect x="218" y="28" width="60" height="34" rx="4" fill="rgba(37,99,235,0.08)" stroke="#1e293b"/><text x="248" y="49" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">EMP</text><path d="M278 45 l10 0 m-4 -3 l4 3 -4 3" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.1" fill="none"/><rect x="288" y="28" width="60" height="34" rx="4" fill="rgba(37,99,235,0.08)" stroke="#1e293b"/><text x="318" y="44" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">Review &</text><text x="318" y="55" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">clearance</text></svg>
figure.caption: Sequential stages of the EIA process.

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Public consultation (public hearing + written responses) is a statutory stage for most Category A and B1 projects under EIA Notification 2006.

**Working.**
Public hearing is mandatory.

**Answer.** Public consultation is mandatory

questions.subject: Environmental Management & Ethics
questions.stem: An uncompensated environmental cost imposed on a third party by an economic activity is called a(n)
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Pollution damage to others is a negative externality the polluter-pays principle internalises.

**Working.**
Negative externality.

**Answer.** Negative externality

questions.subject: Solid & Hazardous Waste Management
questions.stem: Wet MSW $2000\,\text{kg}$ at $60\%$ moisture. The dry-solids mass is _____ kg.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Dry = wet × (1 − moisture).

**Working.**
$$2000(1-60/100)=800.$$

**Answer.** $800\,\text{kg}$

questions.subject: Water & Wastewater Treatment
questions.stem: In the settling-column test plotted in the figure, a particle settles the marked depth in the marked time. Its settling velocity is _____ mm/s.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 330 200" width="100%" style="max-width:350px;height:auto"><line x1="46" y1="20" x2="46" y2="170" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="46" y1="20" x2="300" y2="20" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="6" y="100" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">depth ↓</text><text x="252" y="14" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">time →</text><line x1="46" y1="20" x2="270" y2="150" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.8"/><circle cx="270" cy="150" r="3" fill="#dc2626"/><line x1="270" y1="20" x2="270" y2="150" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><line x1="46" y1="150" x2="270" y2="150" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="150" y="14" font-size="9" fill="#16a34a">t = 300 s</text><text x="118" y="165" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">settles 1.5 m</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** v_s = depth ÷ time (read both from the trajectory).

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{1.5\,\text{m}}{300\,\text{s}}\times1000=5\,\text{mm/s}.$$

**Answer.** $5\,\text{mm/s}$

questions.subject: Water & Wastewater Treatment
questions.stem: For the settling tank shown, read the flow $Q$ (MLD) and plan area $A$ from the diagram. The surface overflow rate $Q/A$ is _____ m³/m²/day.

questions.solution:
**Concept.** SOR = Q/A, with Q and A read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{8\times1000}{200}=40.$$

**Answer.** $40$

questions.subject: Air & Noise Pollution
questions.stem: $3$ identical sources each $75\,\text{dB}$. Combined level is _____ dB.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** L = L₁ + 10log n.

**Working.**
$$75+10\log3=79.77.$$

**Answer.** $79.77\,\text{dB}$

questions.subject: Environmental Management & Ethics
questions.stem: A municipality proposes to mine its sole confined aquifer to exhaustion within 15 years to bankroll current expansion, leaving the next generation with no water source. This plan MOST directly violates which pillar of sustainable development?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Exhausting a resource so future generations cannot meet their own needs breaches inter-generational equity — the core of the Brundtland definition.

**Working.**
Map the scenario to the violated principle.

**Answer.** Inter-generational equity

questions.stem: Read the loose and compacted densities from the bar chart. The volume reduction $(1-\rho_1/\rho_2)$ achieved is _____ %.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 188" width="100%" style="max-width:320px;height:auto"><line x1="40" y1="150" x2="280" y2="150" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><rect x="80" y="126" width="50" height="24" fill="#2563eb" opacity="0.8"/><text x="105" y="122" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2563eb">80</text><text x="105" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">loose ρ₁</text><rect x="180" y="30" width="50" height="120" fill="#16a34a" opacity="0.85"/><text x="205" y="26" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">400</text><text x="205" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">compacted ρ₂</text><text x="160" y="182" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">kg/m³</text></svg>

**Concept.** VR = (1 − ρ₁/ρ₂)×100, both densities read from the bars.

$$\left(1-\dfrac{80}{400}\right)\times100=80\%.$$

ESP.

questions.subject: Water & Wastewater Treatment
questions.stem: Compared with a slow sand filter, a rapid sand filter is characterised by
options: higher filtration rate and reliance on prior coagulation
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Rapid sand filters run at ~100–125 times the slow-filter rate, need coagulation–sedimentation upstream, and are cleaned by backwashing.

**Working.**
Higher rate, needs coagulation.

**Answer.** High rate, coagulation-dependent

questions.subject: Solid & Hazardous Waste Management
questions.stem: Read the loose and compacted densities from the bar chart. The volume reduction $(1-\rho_1/\rho_2)$ achieved is _____ %.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 188" width="100%" style="max-width:320px;height:auto"><line x1="40" y1="150" x2="280" y2="150" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><rect x="80" y="134" width="50" height="16" fill="#2563eb" opacity="0.8"/><text x="105" y="130" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2563eb">80</text><text x="105" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">loose ρ₁</text><rect x="180" y="30" width="50" height="120" fill="#16a34a" opacity="0.85"/><text x="205" y="26" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">600</text><text x="205" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">compacted ρ₂</text><text x="160" y="182" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">kg/m³</text></svg>
figure.caption: Loose and compacted densities shown on the bars only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** VR = (1 − ρ₁/ρ₂)×100, both densities read from the bars.

**Working.**
$$\left(1-\dfrac{80}{600}\right)\times100=86.7\%.$$

**Answer.** $86.7\%$

questions.subject: Air & Noise Pollution
questions.stem: $2$ identical sources each $85\,\text{dB}$. Combined level is _____ dB.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** L = L₁ + 10log n.

**Working.**
$$85+10\log2=88.01.$$

**Answer.** $88.01\,\text{dB}$

questions.subject: Water & Wastewater Treatment
questions.stem: For the settling tank shown, read the flow $Q$ (MLD) and plan area $A$ from the diagram. The surface overflow rate $Q/A$ is _____ m³/m²/day.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 340 170" width="100%" style="max-width:360px;height:auto"><rect x="40" y="40" width="250" height="90" fill="rgba(37,99,235,0.08)" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="1.3"/><path d="M20 60 l20 0 m-5 -3 l5 3 -5 3" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.2" fill="none"/><text x="14" y="55" font-size="9" fill="#16a34a">Q = 6 MLD</text><path d="M290 60 l20 0 m-5 -3 l5 3 -5 3" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.2" fill="none"/><text x="300" y="55" font-size="9" fill="#16a34a">out</text><line x1="40" y1="48" x2="290" y2="48" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><circle cx="120" cy="80" r="2" fill="#1e293b"/><circle cx="170" cy="100" r="2" fill="#1e293b"/><circle cx="210" cy="118" r="2" fill="#1e293b"/><path d="M120 86 L132 124 M170 106 L182 126" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="0.8"/><text x="120" y="150" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">plan area A = 300 m²</text><text x="160" y="36" font-size="8" fill="#2563eb">surface (overflow)</text></svg>
figure.caption: Flow Q and plan area A marked on the diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** SOR = Q/A, with Q and A read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{6\times1000}{300}=20.$$

**Answer.** $20$

questions.subject: Solid & Hazardous Waste Management
questions.stem: Wet MSW $1000\,\text{kg}$ at $50\%$ moisture. The dry-solids mass is _____ kg.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Dry = wet × (1 − moisture).

**Working.**
$$1000(1-50/100)=500.$$

**Answer.** $500\,\text{kg}$

questions.subject: Air & Noise Pollution
questions.stem: At a receptor, $3$ identical machines each produce $85\,\text{dB}$. The combined sound pressure level is _____ dB.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $L=L_1+10\log_{10}n$ for $n$ equal incoherent sources.

**Working.**
$$85+10\log_{10}3=89.77.$$

**Answer.** $89.77\,\text{dB}$

questions.subject: Water & Wastewater Treatment
questions.stem: Ammonia-bearing water is chlorinated. From the breakpoint-chlorination curve shown, read the breakpoint dose. To leave a free residual of $1.5\,\text{mg/L}$, the chlorine that must be applied is _____ mg/L.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 330 195" width="100%" style="max-width:350px;height:auto"><line x1="38" y1="20" x2="38" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="38" y1="165" x2="312" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="4" y="30" font-size="8" fill="#1e293b">residual</text><text x="232" y="182" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">Cl₂ dose (mg/L) →</text><path d="M38 165 L95 120 L150 150 L205 160 L300 80" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.7"/><line x1="205" y1="55" x2="205" y2="165" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><circle cx="205" cy="160" r="3" fill="#dc2626"/><text x="170" y="50" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">breakpoint</text><text x="186" y="178" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">6</text><text x="250" y="92" font-size="7" fill="#16a34a">free residual</text></svg>
figure.caption: Breakpoint dose marked on the x-axis only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Beyond the breakpoint, extra chlorine appears as free residual: applied = breakpoint dose + desired free residual.

**Working.**
$$6+1.5=7.5.$$

**Answer.** $7.5\,\text{mg/L}$

questions.subject: Water & Wastewater Treatment
questions.stem: $L_0=250\,\text{mg/L}$, $k=0.2\,\text{d}^{-1}$ (base e). BOD exerted in $6$ d is _____ mg/L.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 340 200" width="100%" style="max-width:360px;height:auto"><line x1="34" y1="30" x2="34" y2="175" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="34" y1="175" x2="330" y2="175" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="8" y="40" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">DO</text><text x="305" y="190" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">distance/time →</text><line x1="34" y1="45" x2="330" y2="45" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="4 3"/><text x="250" y="42" font-size="8" fill="#16a34a">saturation DO</text><path d="M34 60 Q120 175 175 150 Q260 110 330 70" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.7"/><line x1="175" y1="45" x2="175" y2="150" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><circle cx="175" cy="150" r="3" fill="#dc2626"/><text x="180" y="120" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">D_c = 75 mg/L</text><text x="150" y="170" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">t_c = 3.5 d</text></svg>
figure.caption: Oxygen-sag curve: critical deficit D_c at critical time t_c.

questions.subject: Environmental Microbiology
questions.stem: A culture follows Monod kinetics at substrate $S=40\,\text{mg/L}$. Reading $\mu_{max}$ and $K_s$ from the $\mu$–$S$ curve shown, the specific growth rate $\mu$ is _____ h⁻¹.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 330 195" width="100%" style="max-width:350px;height:auto"><line x1="44" y1="20" x2="44" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="44" y1="165" x2="312" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="240" y="182" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">substrate [S] →</text><line x1="44" y1="40" x2="312" y2="40" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="4 3"/><text x="232" y="36" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">μ_max = 0.6</text><path d="M44 165 C90 110 140 70 200 56 C250 46 290 42 312 41" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.8"/><line x1="44" y1="102" x2="120" y2="102" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><line x1="120" y1="102" x2="120" y2="165" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="14" y="106" font-size="7" fill="#16a34a">μ_max/2</text><text x="98" y="178" font-size="10" fill="#16a34a">K_s = 20</text></svg>
figure.caption: Monod μ–S curve: μ_max (plateau) and K_s (half-saturation) marked on the curve only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** μ = μ_max S/(K_s+S), with μ_max and K_s read from the curve.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{0.6\times40}{20+40}=0.4.$$

**Answer.** $0.4$

questions.solution:
**Concept.** F/M = QS₀/(VX).

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{10000\times250}{2500\times3500}=0.286.$$

questions.subject: Air & Noise Pollution
questions.stem: An ESP treats $8\,\text{m}^3/\text{s}$ of flue gas. Reading the collecting area $A$ and the drift velocity $w$ from the figure, its Deutsch–Anderson collection efficiency is _____ %.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 340 170" width="100%" style="max-width:360px;height:auto"><rect x="40" y="30" width="260" height="110" fill="none" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="1.2"/><line x1="80" y1="30" x2="80" y2="140" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="3"/><line x1="150" y1="30" x2="150" y2="140" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="3"/><line x1="220" y1="30" x2="220" y2="140" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="3"/><line x1="115" y1="35" x2="115" y2="135" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="2 2"/><line x1="185" y1="35" x2="185" y2="135" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="2 2"/><path d="M14 70 l22 0 m-6 -3 l6 3 -6 3" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.2" fill="none"/><text x="6" y="64" font-size="9" fill="#16a34a">gas in</text><text x="60" y="158" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">collecting area A = 300 m²</text><text x="150" y="22" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">w = 0.12 m/s (drift)</text></svg>
figure.caption: Collecting area A and drift velocity w marked on the diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Deutsch–Anderson: $\eta=1-e^{-wA/Q}$, with $A$ and $w$ read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$\left(1-e^{-0.12\times300/8}\right)\times100=98.89\%.$$

**Answer.** $98.89\%$

questions.subject: Solid & Hazardous Waste Management

questions.stem: In the settling-column test plotted in the figure, a particle settles the marked depth in the marked time. Its settling velocity is _____ mm/s.

questions.solution:
**Concept.** v_s = depth ÷ time (read both from the trajectory).

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{3\,\text{m}}{300\,\text{s}}\times1000=10\,\text{mm/s}.$$

questions.subject: Environmental Management & Ethics
questions.stem: An abatement benefit arrives as the single future cash flow shown in the diagram. At a discount rate of $8\%$, its present worth is $₹$ _____.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 330 150" width="100%" style="max-width:350px;height:auto"><line x1="30" y1="100" x2="310" y2="100" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.4"/><text x="30" y="118" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">0</text><line x1="290" y1="100" x2="290" y2="48" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.6"/><path d="M290 46 l-5 9 10 0 z" fill="#16a34a"/><text x="290" y="40" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">F = ₹1,00,000</text><text x="290" y="118" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">year 5</text><text x="165" y="118" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">time (years) →</text><text x="42" y="60" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">PW = ?</text></svg>
figure.caption: Future benefit F and its time horizon n marked on the cash-flow diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Present worth $=\dfrac{F}{(1+i)^n}$, with $F$ and $n$ read from the cash-flow diagram.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{100000}{(1+0.08)^{5}}=68058.$$

**Answer.** $₹68058$

questions.subject: Solid & Hazardous Waste Management
questions.stem: Modified Dulong $HHV=337C+1419(H-O/8)$ kJ/kg with $C=50\%$, $H=6\%$, $O=35\%$. HHV is _____ kJ/kg.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 180" width="100%" style="max-width:320px;height:auto"><line x1="40" y1="150" x2="280" y2="150" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><rect x="70" y="30" width="46" height="120" fill="#dc2626" opacity="0.75"/><text x="93" y="26" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#dc2626">in 19.2</text><rect x="190" y="146" width="46" height="4" fill="#16a34a" opacity="0.8"/><text x="213" y="142" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">out 0</text><text x="160" y="170" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">HHV (MJ/kg)</text></svg>
figure.caption: Energy content of the waste fuel.

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Apply the modified Dulong formula.

**Working.**
$$337(50)+1419(6-35/8)=19156.$$

**Answer.** $19156\,\text{kJ/kg}$

questions.subject: Water & Wastewater Treatment
questions.stem: $L_0=200\,\text{mg/L}$, $k=0.1\,\text{d}^{-1}$ (base e). BOD exerted in $2$ d is _____ mg/L.

questions.subject: Environmental Microbiology
questions.stem: A culture follows Monod kinetics at substrate $S=80\,\text{mg/L}$. Reading $\mu_{max}$ and $K_s$ from the $\mu$–$S$ curve shown, the specific growth rate $\mu$ is _____ h⁻¹.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 330 195" width="100%" style="max-width:350px;height:auto"><line x1="44" y1="20" x2="44" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="44" y1="165" x2="312" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="240" y="182" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">substrate [S] →</text><line x1="44" y1="40" x2="312" y2="40" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="4 3"/><text x="232" y="36" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">μ_max = 0.4</text><path d="M44 165 C90 110 140 70 200 56 C250 46 290 42 312 41" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.8"/><line x1="44" y1="102" x2="120" y2="102" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><line x1="120" y1="102" x2="120" y2="165" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="14" y="106" font-size="7" fill="#16a34a">μ_max/2</text><text x="98" y="178" font-size="10" fill="#16a34a">K_s = 50</text></svg>
figure.caption: Monod μ–S curve: μ_max (plateau) and K_s (half-saturation) marked on the curve only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** μ = μ_max S/(K_s+S), with μ_max and K_s read from the curve.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{0.4\times80}{50+80}=0.246.$$

**Answer.** $0.246$

questions.subject: Solid & Hazardous Waste Management
questions.stem: Read the loose and compacted densities from the bar chart. The volume reduction $(1-\rho_1/\rho_2)$ is _____ %.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 188" width="100%" style="max-width:320px;height:auto"><line x1="40" y1="150" x2="280" y2="150" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><rect x="80" y="129.4" width="50" height="20.6" fill="#2563eb" opacity="0.8"/><text x="105" y="125.4" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2563eb">120</text><text x="105" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">loose ρ₁</text><rect x="180" y="30" width="50" height="120" fill="#16a34a" opacity="0.85"/><text x="205" y="26" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">700</text><text x="205" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">compacted ρ₂</text><text x="160" y="182" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">kg/m³</text></svg>
figure.caption: Loose and compacted densities shown on the bars only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** VR = (1 − ρ₁/ρ₂)×100, both densities read from the bars.

**Working.**
$$\left(1-\dfrac{120}{700}\right)\times100=82.9\%.$$

**Answer.** $82.9\%$

questions.subject: Water & Wastewater Treatment
questions.stem: A discrete particle ($\rho_s=2650\,\text{kg/m}^3$) settles in water ($\rho=1000$, $\mu=10^{-3}\,\text{Pa·s}$). Reading its diameter from the figure, its terminal velocity is _____ ×10⁻³ m/s.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 180" width="100%" style="max-width:320px;height:auto"><rect x="60" y="20" width="180" height="140" fill="rgba(37,99,235,0.06)" stroke="#1e293b"/><circle cx="150" cy="66" r="12" fill="#94a3b8" stroke="#1e293b"/><line x1="138" y1="66" x2="162" y2="66" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1"/><text x="168" y="70" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">d = 0.02 mm</text><path d="M150 82 l0 52 m-4 -6 l4 6 4 -6" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.1" fill="none"/><text x="158" y="118" font-size="9" fill="#16a34a">v_s</text><text x="150" y="174" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">ρ_s = 2650 kg/m³, water μ = 10⁻³ Pa·s</text></svg>
figure.caption: Particle diameter marked on the diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** $v_s=\dfrac{g(\rho_s-\rho)d^2}{18\mu}$, with $d$ read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{9.81\times1650\times(0.00002)^2}{18\times10^{-3}}=0.00036\,\text{m/s}.$$

**Answer.** $0.36\times10^{-3}$

questions.subject: Air & Noise Pollution
questions.stem: An ESP treats $4\,\text{m}^3/\text{s}$. Reading the collecting area $A$ and drift velocity $w$ from the figure, its collection efficiency is _____ %.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 340 170" width="100%" style="max-width:360px;height:auto"><rect x="40" y="30" width="260" height="110" fill="none" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="1.2"/><line x1="80" y1="30" x2="80" y2="140" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="3"/><line x1="150" y1="30" x2="150" y2="140" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="3"/><line x1="220" y1="30" x2="220" y2="140" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="3"/><line x1="115" y1="35" x2="115" y2="135" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="2 2"/><line x1="185" y1="35" x2="185" y2="135" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="2 2"/><path d="M14 70 l22 0 m-6 -3 l6 3 -6 3" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.2" fill="none"/><text x="6" y="64" font-size="9" fill="#16a34a">gas in</text><text x="60" y="158" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">collecting area A = 200 m²</text><text x="150" y="22" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">w = 0.08 m/s (drift)</text></svg>
figure.caption: Collecting area A and drift velocity w marked on the diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** η = 1 − e^{−wA/Q}, with A and w read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$(1-e^{-0.08\times200/4})\times100=98.17\%.$$

**Answer.** $98.17\%$

questions.subject: Global & Regional Environmental Issues
questions.stem: A source emits $18\,\text{t}$ of a greenhouse gas. Reading the gas's GWP₁₀₀ from the figure, its CO₂-equivalent emission is _____ t CO₂-e.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 130" width="100%" style="max-width:300px;height:auto"><rect x="40" y="36" width="220" height="56" rx="6" fill="rgba(217,119,6,0.12)" stroke="#1e293b"/><text x="150" y="58" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">the gas</text><text x="150" y="80" font-size="12" text-anchor="middle" fill="#dc2626">GWP₁₀₀ = 265</text><text x="150" y="112" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">CO₂-e = mass × GWP</text></svg>
figure.caption: Global-warming potential of the gas marked on the diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** CO₂-e = mass × GWP, with the GWP read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$18\times265=4770.$$

**Answer.** $4770$

questions.subject: Air & Noise Pollution
questions.stem: A stack emits $100\,\text{g/s}$ of a pollutant. Using the effective height $H$, wind speed $u$ and dispersion coefficients $\sigma_y,\sigma_z$ marked on the diagram, the ground-level concentration on the plume centreline (full ground reflection, $y=0$) is _____ ×10⁻⁴ g/m³.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 360 210" width="100%" style="max-width:380px;height:auto"><line x1="20" y1="175" x2="350" y2="175" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.4"/><rect x="34" y="75" width="14" height="100" fill="#94a3b8" stroke="#1e293b"/><text x="41" y="191" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">stack</text><path d="M48 75 Q160 64 350 44 M48 75 Q160 86 350 116" fill="rgba(37,99,235,0.10)" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="41" y1="75" x2="41" y2="175" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="54" y="70" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">H = 50 m</text><path d="M118 148 l20 0 m-5 -3 l5 3 -5 3" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.1" fill="none"/><text x="142" y="151" font-size="10" fill="#16a34a">u = 5 m/s</text><text x="206" y="40" font-size="10" fill="#2563eb">σ_y = 30 m,  σ_z = 20 m</text><text x="318" y="171" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">x →</text></svg>
figure.caption: Elevated point source: H, u, σ_y and σ_z are marked on the diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** $C=\dfrac{Q}{\pi u\sigma_y\sigma_z}\exp\!\left(-\dfrac{H^2}{2\sigma_z^2}\right)$ (ground, centreline, full reflection); $H,u,\sigma_y,\sigma_z$ read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{100}{\pi\times5\times30\times20}e^{-50^2/(2\times20^2)}=0.00046619\,\text{g/m}^3.$$

**Answer.** $4.662\times10^{-4}$

questions.subject: Environmental Chemistry
questions.stem: Read the Ca²⁺ and Mg²⁺ concentrations from the bar chart. The total hardness as CaCO₃ (eq. wt Ca 20, Mg 12.15) is _____ mg/L.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 188" width="100%" style="max-width:320px;height:auto"><line x1="40" y1="150" x2="280" y2="150" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><rect x="80" y="50" width="50" height="100" fill="#2563eb" opacity="0.8"/><text x="105" y="46" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2563eb">20</text><text x="105" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">Ca²⁺</text><rect x="180" y="30" width="50" height="120" fill="#16a34a" opacity="0.85"/><text x="205" y="26" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">24</text><text x="205" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">Mg²⁺</text><text x="160" y="182" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">mg/L</text></svg>
figure.caption: Ca²⁺ and Mg²⁺ concentrations shown on the bars only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Convert each ion to CaCO₃ (×50/eq.wt) and sum, with both concentrations read from the bars.

**Working.**
$$20\times\dfrac{50}{20}+24\times\dfrac{50}{12.15}=148.8.$$

**Answer.** $148.8$

questions.subject: Global & Regional Environmental Issues
questions.stem: A source emits $3\,\text{t}$ of a greenhouse gas. Reading the gas's GWP₁₀₀ from the figure, its CO₂-equivalent emission is _____ t CO₂-e.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 130" width="100%" style="max-width:300px;height:auto"><rect x="40" y="36" width="220" height="56" rx="6" fill="rgba(217,119,6,0.12)" stroke="#1e293b"/><text x="150" y="58" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">the gas</text><text x="150" y="80" font-size="12" text-anchor="middle" fill="#dc2626">GWP₁₀₀ = 28</text><text x="150" y="112" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">CO₂-e = mass × GWP</text></svg>
figure.caption: Global-warming potential of the gas marked on the diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** CO₂-e = mass × GWP, with the GWP read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$3\times28=84.$$

**Answer.** $84$

questions.subject: Air & Noise Pollution
questions.stem: A stack emits $Q=100\,\text{g/s}$. Using the effective height $H$, wind speed $u$ and dispersion coefficients $\sigma_y,\sigma_z$ marked on the diagram, the ground-level centreline concentration (full reflection) is _____ ×10⁻⁴ g/m³.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 360 210" width="100%" style="max-width:380px;height:auto"><line x1="20" y1="175" x2="350" y2="175" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.4"/><rect x="34" y="75" width="14" height="100" fill="#94a3b8" stroke="#1e293b"/><text x="41" y="191" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">stack</text><path d="M48 75 Q160 64 350 44 M48 75 Q160 86 350 116" fill="rgba(37,99,235,0.10)" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="41" y1="75" x2="41" y2="175" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="54" y="70" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">H = 60 m</text><path d="M118 148 l20 0 m-5 -3 l5 3 -5 3" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.1" fill="none"/><text x="142" y="151" font-size="10" fill="#16a34a">u = 3 m/s</text><text x="206" y="40" font-size="10" fill="#2563eb">σ_y = 30 m,  σ_z = 15 m</text><text x="318" y="171" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">x →</text></svg>
figure.caption: Elevated point source: H, u, σ_y and σ_z are marked on the diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** C = Q/(πuσ_yσ_z)·exp(−H²/2σ_z²); H,u,σ_y,σ_z read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{100}{\pi\times3\times30\times15}e^{-60^2/(2\times15^2)}=0.00000791.$$

**Answer.** $0.079\times10^{-4}$

questions.subject: Air & Noise Pollution
questions.stem: Two sources $68\,\text{dB}$ and $76\,\text{dB}$ at a point. Combined level is _____ dB.
